column3: IANA registry for "QUIC Transport Error Codes" under QUIC heading
column5: Section is an IANA registry definition; YAWT does not maintain a registry (this is IANA's responsibility)

column3: 62-bit error code space governed by registry
column5: Error codes are stored as uint64_t (variable-length integer, up to 62 bits) in CONNECTION_CLOSE and RESET_STREAM frame structs

column3: Three policy ranges in error code space (Specification Required vs Standards Action)
column5: Section is an IANA policy definition; codebase does not implement registration policies

column3: Required registration fields: Code and Description
column5: Section is an IANA registry field definition, not a protocol implementation requirement
Error codes are parsed and serialized as raw varint values but no specific NO_ERROR constant or handling is defined in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for INTERNAL_ERROR (0x01) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for CONNECTION_REFUSED (0x02) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR (0x03) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for STREAM_LIMIT_ERROR (0x04) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for STREAM_STATE_ERROR (0x05) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for FINAL_SIZE_ERROR (0x06) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR (0x07) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR (0x08) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for CONNECTION_ID_LIMIT_ERROR (0x09) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for PROTOCOL_VIOLATION (0x0a) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for INVALID_TOKEN (0x0b) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for APPLICATION_ERROR (0x0c) in YAWT
Defined as YAWT_Q_ERR_CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED in quic.h and handled in quic_connection.c (line 375-377) with CONNECTION_CLOSE(0x0d, 0x06)
No constant or handling defined for KEY_UPDATE_ERROR (0x0e) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for AEAD_LIMIT_REACHED (0x0f) in YAWT
No constant or handling defined for NO_VIABLE_PATH (0x10) in YAWT
No mapping from TLS alert codes to CRYPTO_ERROR range defined in YAWT

column3: CONNECTION_CLOSE frames carry error codes as variable-length integers
column5: Parsed with YAWT_q_varint_decode and encoded with YAWT_q_varint_encode in quic.c

column3: Both CONNECTION_CLOSE (transport) and CONNECTION_CLOSE (application) frame types support error codes
column5: Two distinct frame types YAWT_Q_FRAME_CONNECTION_CLOSE (0x1c) and YAWT_Q_FRAME_CONNECTION_CLOSE_APP (0x1d) both include error_code fields

column3: RESET_STREAM frames carry application error codes
column5: YAWT_Q_Frame_Reset_Stream_t struct includes app_error_code field

column3: STOP_SENDING frames carry application error codes
column5: YAWT_Q_Frame_Stop_Sending_t struct includes app_error_code field
